TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan kati Tjukutjuku (Kuɫaɫau village, p.227)

S.FORM: izua ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a se Ɫinasalas; saka izua ti Tjukutjuku a se Makalaulauz.
S.PHON: izua ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a sə ʎinasaɭas; saka izua ti cukucuku a sə makaɭauɭauz.
S.FORM: izua ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a se Ljinasalas; saka izua ti Tjukutjuku a se Makalaulauz.
S.PHON: izua ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a sə ʎinasaɭas; saka izua ti ʦukuʦuku a sə makaɭauɭauz.
S.TRANSL: There was a person called Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan of Ɫinasalas, and also Tjukutjuku of Makalaulauz.

S.FORM: "ari, qaɫupi," aya tua sikataqaɫan; sa vaik a sema qemaɫup.
S.PHON: "ari, qaʎupi," aja tua sikataqaʎan; sa vaik a səma qəmaʎup.
S.FORM: "ari, qaljupi," aya tua sikataqaljan; sa vaik a sema qemaljup.
S.PHON: "ari, qaʎupi," aja tua sikataqaʎan; sa vaik a səma qəmaʎup.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go hunting,” said (Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan) to his fellow-villagers; and they went hunting.

S.FORM: manu izua kasiw a qatsaqatsa.
S.PHON: manu izua kasiw a qaʦaqaʦa.
S.FORM: manu izua kasiw a qacaqaca.
S.PHON: manu izua kasiw a qaʦaqaʦa.
S.TRANSL: Now there was a tall tree,

S.FORM: manu ɫevavaw ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: manu ʎəvavaw ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: manu ljevavaw ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: manu ʎəvavaw ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan climbed up it.

S.FORM: manu ɫengɫengen ti Tjukutjuku, a pasa Makalaulauz.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋən ti cukucuku, a pasa makaɭauɭauz.
S.FORM: manu ljengljengen ti Tjukutjuku, a pasa Makalaulauz.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋən ti ʦukuʦuku, a pasa makaɭauɭauz.
S.TRANSL: He saw Tjukutjuku over in Makalaulauz,

S.FORM: manu tjenglay ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: manu cəŋɭaj ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: manu tjenglay ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: manu ʦəŋɭaj ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: and fell in love with her.

S.FORM: "ku kudavaw tsu," aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan; sa sikivuɫu tua quvaɫ tu talayan.
S.PHON: "ku kudavaw ʦu," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; sa sikivuʎu tua quvaʎ tu taɭajan.
S.FORM: "ku kudavaw cu," aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan; sa sikivulju tua quvalj tu talayan.
S.PHON: "ku kudavaw ʦu," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan; sa sikivuʎu tua quvaʎ tu taɭajan.
S.TRANSL: “What shall I do about this?” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan, and pulled out one of his hairs.

S.FORM: manu sipavali, sa djaɫun tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu sipavaɭi, sa ɉaʎun cai cukucuku.
S.FORM: manu sipavali, sa djaljun tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: manu sipavaɭi, sa ɉaʎun ʦai ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: It was carried by the wind all the way to Tjukutjuku,

S.FORM: manu sema tapuɫu.
S.PHON: manu səma tapuʎu.
S.FORM: manu sema tapulju.
S.PHON: manu səma tapuʎu.
S.TRANSL: and went into her lap.

S.FORM: sa kavunglay ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa kavuŋɭaj ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: sa kavunglay ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: sa kavuŋɭaj ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku became pregnant.

S.FORM: makalimaɫ a vunglanglay; puaɫak tu uqaɫay tu ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: makaɭimaʎ a vuŋɭaŋɭaj; puaʎak tu uqaʎaj tu ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: makalimalj a vunglanglay; pualjak tu uqaljay tu ti Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: makaɭimaʎ a vuŋɭaŋɭaj; puaʎak tu uqaʎaj tu ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: She was pregnant for five days, and bore a son called Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.

S.FORM: "aku a puaɫak a paqeteleng a neka nu tsekeɫ?
S.PHON: "aku a puaʎak a paqətəɭəŋ a nəka nu ʦəkəʎ
S.FORM: "aku a pualjak a paqeteleng a neka nu cekelj?
S.PHON: "aku a puaʎak a paqətəɭəŋ a nəka nu ʦəkəʎ
S.TRANSL: “Why has she had a child on the side without being married?

S.FORM: kaɫavaru a temaqaɫan tuki tima a navenunglay."
S.PHON: kaʎavaru a təmaqaʎan tuki tima a navənuŋɭaj."
S.FORM: kaljavaru a temaqaljan tuki tima a navenunglay."
S.PHON: kaʎavaru a təmaqaʎan tuki tima a navənuŋɭaj."
S.TRANSL: All the village must find out who made her pregnant.”

S.FORM: manu neka.
S.PHON: manu nəka.
S.FORM: manu neka.
S.PHON: manu nəka.
S.TRANSL: But noone was found.

S.FORM: "keɫu," ayain ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a se Ɫinasalas.
S.PHON: "kəʎu," ajain ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a sə ʎinasaɭas.
S.FORM: "kelju," ayain ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a se Ljinasalas.
S.PHON: "kəʎu," ajain ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a sə ʎinasaɭas.
S.TRANSL: Then Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan of Ɫinasalas was asked to come.

S.FORM: manu mangtjez ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: manu maŋcəz ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: manu mangtjez ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: manu maŋʦəz ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: So he came.

S.FORM: "pai, ɫa kina, avan tsu a ku kama."
S.PHON: "pai, ʎa kina, avan ʦu a ku kama."
S.FORM: "pai, lja kina, avan cu a ku kama."
S.PHON: "pai, ʎa kina, avan ʦu a ku kama."
S.TRANSL: (The child said:) “Now, mother, this is my father.”

S.FORM: saka "iya, manu avan tsu a kama," aya a temaqaɫan.
S.PHON: saka "ija, manu avan ʦu a kama," aja a təmaqaʎan.
S.FORM: saka "iya, manu avan cu a kama," aya a temaqaljan.
S.PHON: saka "ija, manu avan ʦu a kama," aja a təmaqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: So all the village said: “Well, so this is the father!”

S.FORM: sa pavay anga sakamaya tua sinilisi, sa pareputsekeɫ anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: sa pavaj aŋa sakamaja tua siniɭisi, sa parəpuʦəkəʎ aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: sa pavay anga sakamaya tua sinilisi, sa parepucekelj anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: sa pavaj aŋa sakamaja tua siniɭisi, sa parəpuʦəkəʎ aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: So he gave the bride-price, and they finished by getting married.

S.FORM: saka amin a kai.
S.PHON: saka amin a kai.
S.FORM: saka amin a kai.
S.PHON: saka amin a kai.
S.TRANSL: That’s the end of the story.
